ddocEntries.title: Feature Brief
ddocEntries.content:
# Index Cost — Customer Discovery & SIP Evidence

**Design Ticket:** [SERVICE-4335](https://buildops.atlassian.net/browse/SERVICE-4335)
**Epic:** [SERVICE-4214](https://buildops.atlassian.net/browse/SERVICE-4214)
**SIP Issues:** SIP-8787 · SIP (Index Cost Bulk Updates)

---

## What Customers Are Saying

### SIP: "Ability to Use Index Cost for Pricing on Job Report"
**Customer:** SmartCare (and others adopting Index Cost)

**Problem:**
Job Reports today continue to rely on costs from source purchasing documents (POs, AP Bills) or Inventory Issues — **not** from the Index Cost. This undermines the entire purpose of Index Cost and creates an inconsistency between the pricing strategy set in the Cost Catalogue and what actually appears on the job report.

**Business Impact:**
- Margins are frequently eroded or inconsistently applied
- Pricing accuracy depends on individual vigilance rather than system enforcement
- Teams spend excessive time validating costs and markups instead of higher-value work
- Customers adopting Index Cost do not realize its full value until Job Report uses it

**Current Workaround:**
Supervisors, managers, or billing clerks manually verify that pricing is calculated using the correct cost before invoicing — highly manual and error-prone.

---

### SIP: "Item-Level Index Cost Bulk Updates Applied to Pricebooks"

**Problem:**
Customers need to update pricing for a specific item when its supplier cost changes due to an external index, and apply that change **only to selected pricebooks**. BuildOps does not support targeted, item-level bulk updates tied to index cost changes.

**Business Impact:**
- Manual updates for every high-frequency cost change on indexed items
- Risk of inconsistent pricing across pricebooks when updates are missed

**Current Workaround:**
Manually identify impacted pricebooks, then update the item price one pricebook at a time. Some customers maintain separate tracking outside BuildOps to manage index changes.

**Context:**
- Customers using Inventory, Procurement, and multiple pricebooks tied to different customers/regions/contracts
- Index-based cost changes on individual items are common and recurring — not an edge case
- Under contract with due date **2026-06-30**

---

## Design Scope (SERVICE-4335)

The design ticket covers:
1. **Cost Catalogue UI** — list view, create/edit cost catalogue entries
2. **Job Report integration** — using index cost as markup base (max(Index Cost, Purchase Cost))
3. **Property-level inheritance** — property inherits a Cost Catalogue; job inherits from property
4. **Phase 1 only**: No manual override at job level, no pricebook-level cost override

## What's Built (feature/index-cost branch)
- `CostCatalogueList` UI with mock data (commit c7d322ae)
- Index cost design spec (commit 48f9ea65)
- Component preview: `docs/index-cost-component-preview.html`

Epic: SERVICE-4214 | Feature Flag: cost-catalogues

Problem / Why: When an index cost is no longer appropriate for a specific product, users need to remove the entry so that the product falls back to standard pricing behavior.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • "Restore Default Cost" action on each row in the entry list on the details page

  • Confirmation dialog: "Removing this entry will revert [Product Name] to standard cost-based pricing."

  • On confirm, calls DELETE /cost-catalogues/:id/entries/:entryId (SERVICE-4306); entry removed from the list on success

  • Error toast on failure

  • Component tests: confirmation, confirm action, cancel action, entry removed from list

Dependencies: SERVICE-4306 (DELETE entry), Cost Catalogue details page ticket

Notes / Out of Scope:

  • Restoring archived entries is out of scope for v1

# Bulk PO Creation — Customer Discovery (IPP-326)

**Discovery Call:** [Dec 12, 2025 (Gong)](https://us-1330.app.gong.io/call?id=4381445529237724212)
**Spec:** IPP-326 | **DRI:** Tamara Mekhantseva
**Customers:** Service Logic · Tolin Mechanical · Piedmont Service Group · IES

---

## What We Heard

### Tolin Mechanical (Bobbi Duran, Director Corporate Services; Nick Dosen, CFO)
> *"I generate all of the filters that need to be ordered by contract into an excel spreadsheet... I forward that to our filter company."* — Bobbi Duran

> *"It's a little unreasonable to have us go hand-enter like 3,000 filters. We're going to have to come up with a way to import it."* — Nick Dosen

- **700+ contracts** across Colorado and Arizona
- Orders placed **last week of each month, 6 weeks in advance**
- Single vendor per state (drop-ship, no inventory holding)
- Current process: Agreement Central → Excel → PDF email to vendor → vendor delivers "tagged and bagged by contract number"
- No formal receiving process; vendor accuracy is trusted
- No item-to-equipment mapping; they order by contract totals, not per-unit

### Piedmont Service Group (Kenneth Oesch, CFO)
- 11 divisions, each with different rates ($85, $75, $35, etc.)
- Billers manually look at who was on the call and change the amount on **every single invoice**
- No automation exists — entirely manual across all 11 divisions

### IES / Jason Saunders (Reference Standard)
- One bulk PO action in current ERP generates POs per inventory site
- PO lines include: Property ID, Job ID, Visit date, Material SKU + Qty, Warehouse
- **This is the benchmark BuildOps should match**

---

## Key Discoveries

| Topic | Finding |
|-------|---------|
| Entry point | Maintenance List bulk action (not SA level) — procurement managers work from the list |
| Ordering timing | 6 weeks ahead; scheduling/tech assignment is NOT a prerequisite for ordering |
| Grouping | One PO per vendor (or vendor × department when maintenances span multiple depts) |
| Vendor resolution | Phase 1: item's `preferredVendorId` only; state-based routing deferred to Phase 2 |
| Cost model | Non-inventory, expense-only; PO is non-posting committed cost; receipt ≠ journal entry |
| Intacct | High likelihood bills generated in Intacct via AP automation and read back to BuildOps |
| Import dependency | Tolin has ~3,000 filters — bulk import of task materials is a de facto prerequisite |

---

## What Was NOT Decided

- Bounded limit per bulk operation (Tolin has 700 contracts — needs engineering sizing)
- `includeInBulkPO` propagation model (SA template → generated maintenances)
- Whether to call PO create endpoint N times or build a new bulk endpoint
- Intacct: export on Draft creation or Submitted status?

---

## Prototype

Branch: `feature/bulk-po` — run locally with `git checkout feature/bulk-po && yarn start`

Key commits:
- `251587d7` feat: add bulk PO creation for maintenance — ASSETS-3200
- `ee451b89` fix: show no-eligible-items warning when API returns 0 task entries
- `ffacc9ad` feat: add Create PO bulk action to SA maintenances table

domainKnowledge.title: Service Agreements, Tasks, P&M & Bulk PO Creation
domainKnowledge.content:
### Knowledge Base · Agent Reference

> **Owner:** Ying Zhang (Elaine) · **Updated:** March 2026
> **Use this for:** Understanding the SA domain model, task layers, P&M flow, and the Bulk PO feature (IPP-326) — as agent context or human reference.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [What Is a Service Agreement?](#1-what-is-a-service-agreement)
2. [SA Setup Wizard — 7 Steps](#2-sa-setup-wizard--7-steps)
3. [The Three Task Layers](#3-the-three-task-layers)
4. [Parts & Materials — How They Flow](#4-parts--materials-pm--how-they-flow)
5. [Maintenance Generation](#5-maintenance-generation)
6. [Key Entity Relationships](#6-key-entity-relationships)
7. [Checklist Library — Key Rules](#7-checklist-library--key-rules)
8. [NEW FEATURE: Bulk PO Creation (IPP-326)](#8-new-feature-bulk-po-creation-for-maintenance-jobs-ipp-326)
9. [Bulk PO — Q&A with Engineering/Product](#9-bulk-po--qa-with-engineeringproduct)
10. [Open Questions & Risks](#10-open-questions--risks)
11. [Related Links](#11-related-links)

---

## 1. What Is a Service Agreement?

A **Service Agreement (SA)** — also called a **"contract"** by customers — is the top-level entity representing a recurring maintenance contract between a contractor and a customer.

| Dimension | What it defines |
|---|---|
| **Who** | Customer, billing customer, department |
| **What** | Scope of work — properties, assets, checklists, tasks |
| **When** | Maintenance schedule — interval, frequency, due dates |
| **How much** | Budget, pricing, invoicing rules |

Once activated, the SA **auto-generates Maintenance Jobs** on a recurring schedule based on Step 3 configuration.

### SA Lifecycle

```
DRAFT → ACTIVATING → ACTIVE → EXPIRED
                  ↓
               ERROR  (retry after fix)

ACTIVE → UNDER_REVISION → ACTIVE  (on re-activation)
ACTIVE → CANCELED  (terminal)
```

> ⚠️ **Key rules**
> - Only **DRAFT** and **UNDER_REVISION** allow edits
> - Activation is async (SQS FIFO queue) — state briefly becomes ACTIVATING
> - Changes to an active SA require a **Revision** — revisions do **NOT** retroactively update already-generated maintenances

---

## 2. SA Setup Wizard — 7 Steps

| Step | Name | What Happens |
|---|---|---|
| **1** | Overview & Billing | SA name/number, customer, billing customer, department, SA type, billing type |
| **2** | Properties & Assets | Select properties and assets. Each asset must have a checklist linked. |
| **3** | Create Maintenance Plan | ⭐ Define tasks per asset/property. Set schedule. Add P&M and forms to tasks. **Core step.** |
| **4** | Schedule Maintenances | Group tasks into Maintenance Groups. Define visits, budgeted hours, job type, tech defaults. |
| **5** | Labor Budgets | Set labor cost budgets per property/group. Task-level labor type + rate in progress. |
| **6** | SA Budget | Overall cost budget by month/year. |
| **7** | Invoicing | Recurring invoice schedules, templates, billing amounts, email config. |

---

## 3. The Three Task Layers

Tasks exist at **three distinct levels** — different entities, different mutability, different purposes.

> 💡 **Mental model:** Checklist Task = recipe template → SA Task = recipe customized for this contract → Maintenance Task = the actual dish being made on a specific day.

---

### Layer 1 — Checklist Task
**`TaskTemplate` in the Checklist Library (Settings)**

The global reusable template. The source of truth before any SA-specific context exists.

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **DB entity** | `TaskTemplate` (with `taskChecklistId` → `TaskTemplateChecklist`) |
| **Scoped to** | Property-level checklist OR asset-type checklist |
| **Contains** | Task name, description, interval, occurrence count, forms |
| **P&M** | ❌ Not supported today — known gap |
| **Editable?** | ✅ Freely, until linked to an active SA. Then **blocked.** |
| **Propagates changes?** | ❌ Changes do NOT flow back to SAs already using it |
| **Who uses it** | Contract admins setting up new SAs |

---

### Layer 2 — SA Task
**`TaskTemplate` in a Service Agreement (Step 3)**

A copy/customization of a checklist task, scoped to a specific SA + property/asset combination.

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **DB entity** | `TaskTemplate` (same table, SA-scoped via join tables: `PropertyAssetTaskTemplateChecklist` or `CustomerPropertyTaskTemplateChecklist`) |
| **SA edits tracked in** | `ServiceAgreementTaskTemplateEdit` (edit types: ADD / EDIT / DELETE) |
| **Contains** | Everything in checklist task + P&M (`TaskTemplatePart`), labor estimate, labor type/rate (in progress), forms |
| **P&M** | ✅ Supported — `TaskTemplatePart` (up to 10 per task) |
| **Schedule** | Defines interval + first due date + occurrence count — drives maintenance generation |
| **Editable?** | ✅ While SA is Draft or Under Revision only |
| **Who uses it** | Contract admins in Step 3 wizard |

---

### Layer 3 — Maintenance Task
**`Task` / `JobTask` on a live Maintenance Job**

The actual work unit a technician sees and executes in the field.

| Attribute | Detail |
|---|---|
| **DB entity** | `Task` — completely separate from `TaskTemplate` |
| **Created by** | Maintenance generation pipeline (SA activation → SQS FIFO → GraphQL mutations) |
| **Point-in-time** | Snapshot of the SA Task at generation — NOT updated if the SA changes later |
| **P&M** | ✅ `MaintenanceTaskRequiredPart` — inherited from SA task, overridable per maintenance |
| **Ad-hoc editable?** | ✅ Add/remove tasks on a specific maintenance without affecting the SA or others |
| **Labor** | `TaskBudgetBucket` — in-progress feature for per-task labor rate tracking |
| **Who uses it** | Dispatchers + Technicians in the field |

---

### All Three — Side by Side

| | Checklist Task | SA Task | Maintenance Task |
|---|---|---|---|
| **DB entity** | `TaskTemplate` (library) | `TaskTemplate` (SA-scoped) | `Task` / `JobTask` |
| **When it exists** | Always, in Settings | SA setup (Draft → Active) | After activation, per occurrence |
| **Editable?** | ✅ Until linked to SA | ✅ Draft / Under Revision only | ✅ Ad-hoc, per maintenance |
| **P&M?** | ❌ gap | ✅ `TaskTemplatePart` | ✅ `MaintenanceTaskRequiredPart` |
| **Schedule?** | ✅ interval, occurrences | ✅ refined per SA/property | ❌ resolved — has a due date |
| **Propagates to** | SA Tasks (on import) | Maintenance Tasks (on activation) | Nothing downstream |
| **Who uses it** | Contract admins (SA setup) | Contract admins (Step 3) | Dispatchers + Technicians |

---

## 4. Parts & Materials (P&M) — How They Flow

### Where P&M Can Live Today

| Level | Supported? | Entity | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Checklist Library | ❌ | — | Known gap. No default P&M on library tasks. |
| SA level (overall) | ❌ directly | — | Workaround: bulk-add same items to all tasks |
| **SA Task (Step 3)** | **✅** | `TaskTemplatePart` | **Primary place.** Up to 10 items per task. |
| Maintenance Task | ✅ | `MaintenanceTaskRequiredPart` | Inherited from SA task. Overridable per maintenance. |
| Asset item associations | ⚠️ | — | Surface-level only — doesn't auto-flow anywhere |

### How to Add P&M to a Task (Step 3)

**With bulk edit FF enabled:**
- **Single task** → "Items" column → `+Add` → select from dropdown
- **Multiple tasks** → Select via checkboxes → Bulk Actions → Add Items → select items

**Without FF:** Step 3 → select checklist in left nav → gear icon on task → add items

### Key Rules
- ✅ Items must already exist in the account's item list
- ✅ Adding P&M in Step 3 does **NOT** trigger new maintenance generation (not part of the maintenance hash)
- ✅ On activation, tasks and their P&M are stamped onto generated maintenances

---

## 5. Maintenance Generation

### Triggers

| Trigger | Description |
|---|---|
| **SA Activation** | Generates maintenances for the upcoming window |
| **Daily scheduled job** | Async job that generates maintenances for SAs with upcoming due dates |

### Maintenance Hash (Identity Key)

```
{SA_ID}_{PROPERTY_ID}_{DATE}_{TASK_ID_1}...{TASK_ID_N}
```

A **new maintenance is generated** when this hash changes for a given date.

| ✅ Triggers new maintenance | ❌ Does NOT trigger new maintenance |
|---|---|
| Add / remove asset | Adding parts or forms to tasks in Step 3 |
| Add / remove task for any asset | Changing bill-on-completion details |
| Change interval on a task (for affected dates) | Changing maintenance group data (Step 4) |
| | Changing SA budget |

### Revisions

> ⚠️ Existing maintenances are **never affected** by SA revisions — only future ones.

- Recommended maintenance generation window: **30–90 days** ahead
- Fields **locked** once a draft is created: **Start Date**, **Customer**

---

## 6. Key Entity Relationships

```
ServiceAgreement
  ├── Customer (billing + service)
  ├── CustomerProperty (1:N)
  │     └── PropertyAsset (equipment)
  │           └── TaskTemplateChecklist (per-asset checklists)
  ├── TaskTemplate (1:N — work definitions)
  │     ├── TaskSchedule (interval + occurrence count)
  │     ├── TaskTemplatePart ← P&M lives here (SA task level)
  │     └── Forms (legacy + Joyfill v3)
  ├── AdvancedMaintenanceTemplateGroup (Step 4 grouping)
  │     └── VisitTemplate
  ├── RecurrenceDefinition (billing frequency per property)
  ├── InvoiceTemplate (recurring invoice line items)
  ├── BudgetBucket (per-property budget allocation)
  └── MaintenancePreview (planned future maintenance, pre-activation)
            ↓ on activation
  Job (= Maintenance Job)
    └── Task (= Maintenance Task)
          ├── MaintenanceTaskRequiredPart ← P&M at maintenance level
          └── TaskBudgetBucket (labor rate — in progress)
```

---

## 7. Checklist Library — Key Rules

- Checklists are created in **Settings before** an SA is set up
- Two types:
  - **Property-level** — work at the site regardless of specific equipment
  - **Asset-level** — tied to a specific asset type (e.g., RTU, Boiler)
- ❌ Cannot be edited once linked to an active SA
- ❌ Edits do not propagate back to SAs already using the checklist
- ✅ Can be cloned or archived at any time

**Workaround for edits:** Clone checklist → make edits → reassign via Step 3 (or Step 3 bulk import)

> A checklist is **"locked"** once it has a `CustomerPropertyTaskTemplateChecklist` or `PropertyAssetTaskTemplateChecklist` row pointing to it — any import attempt will be rejected.

---

## 8. NEW FEATURE: Bulk PO Creation for Maintenance Jobs (IPP-326)

### Problem Statement

High-volume service contractors (e.g., Service Logic / Tolin Mechanical) order consumables — filters, belts — in bulk by contract, monthly, typically **6 weeks in advance**. Today:

- BuildOps only supports POs **one-by-one per maintenance** — doesn't scale
- Tolin manages **700+ contracts** across CO and AZ — manually impossible
- Customers maintain **parallel spreadsheets** outside BuildOps
- ~3,000 filters would need manual entry without import capability

> 💰 **Revenue at stake:** Service Logic ARR $1.6M + Mac Miller ARR $2.6M

---

### End-to-End Workflow (Demoed March 5, 2026)

#### Step 1 — Tag items as eligible for bulk PO

A new boolean flag: **`includeInBulkPO`**

| Version | Where the flag lives |
|---|---|
| **As demoed** | Master item list → propagates down to checklist task → SA task → maintenance task |
| **Cycle 0 spec (latest)** | `TaskMaterial` on the SA/maintenance, also settable in the Checklist Library |

- Default: `false`
- Overridable at any level (e.g., skip bulk ordering for one specific maintenance)
- Typical use: filters and belts = `true`; larger custom parts = `false`

---

#### Step 2 — Filter Maintenances

1. Go to **Maintenance List**
2. Filter by **due date** (e.g., next month) and/or **Service Agreement** ("contract" in customer language)
3. Bulk-select the relevant maintenances

---

#### Step 3 — Trigger Bulk Action

> **Bulk Actions → "Create Purchase Orders"**

---

#### Step 4 — Review Confirmation Dialog

The dialog shows:
- All parts pre-filtered by `includeInBulkPO = true`
- Quantities from task-level P&M
- Default vendors auto-suggested from `Product.preferredVendorId`
- Proposed PO grouping — each group shown as a separate subsection
- User can override vendors, split lines, or add items before confirming

---

#### Step 5 — PO Grouping Logic

POs are split/grouped by **three dimensions**:

---

##### Dimension 1 — Vendor

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Primary source** | Item's `Product.preferredVendorId` |
| **Fallback** | Department Preferred Vendor mapping (Procurement Settings) |
| **No vendor found** | Line excluded + user warned |
| **Override** | User can change vendor per line in the confirmation dialog |
| **Multi-vendor split** | A single item can split across vendors if one supplier can't fulfill full volume |

---

##### Dimension 2 — Vendor Location

BuildOps supports **multiple locations per vendor** (IP-1002 — in production). Each location has its own billing address, shipping address, and contact.

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Default** | Vendor's default location (`Vendor.defaultLocationId`) |
| **Ideal (Phase 2)** | Auto-suggest the location whose state matches the property's state |
| **Phase 1** | User overrides location in the confirmation dialog |
| **Why it matters** | Large vendors (Trane, Carrier, Ferguson) have locations across the US — the right location determines who to contact and where the order ships from |

---

##### Dimension 3 — Shipping Destination

| Option | Typical use |
|---|---|
| **Warehouse** | ⭐ Most common for bulk PM parts — deliver ahead of time, sorted by property for tech pickup ("tagged and bagged") |
| Vendor Location | Pick up from vendor's location |
| Job / Project Site | Deliver directly to the property address |
| Manually entered | One-off address |

---

##### Grouping Logic in Practice

```
If vendorId + vendorLocationId + shippingDestination are all the same
  → Lines are grouped into ONE PO

If any of those differ
  → Lines are split into SEPARATE POs
```

**Concrete example:**
10 maintenances across 3 properties, all using Ferguson as the filter vendor:

| Maintenances | State | Vendor Location | Ships to | Result |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 7 maintenances | Colorado | Ferguson Denver | CO warehouse | **1 PO** |
| 3 maintenances | Arizona | Ferguson Phoenix | AZ warehouse | **1 PO** |
| **Total** | | | | **2 POs** — each with multiple lines, each line tagged to its maintenance job |

---

#### Step 6 — Confirm → Draft POs Created

- All POs generated in **Draft status** for further review, approval, and receiving
- **Committed costs** tracked at PO creation; **actual costs** on receipt/bill
- New **"PO Status" column** added to:
  - Maintenance List
  - SA → Maintenances tab

---

### Phase 1 Scope

| ✅ In Scope | ❌ Out of Scope (Phase 1) |
|---|---|
| "Create Purchase Orders" bulk action on Maintenance List | Automated / scheduled PO generation |
| `includeInBulkPO` flag on task materials (SA + Checklist Library), default false, per-maintenance override | Bill of Materials view per maintenance |
| Confirmation dialog previewing POs grouped by vendor | Vendor-by-state / location configuration |
| POs in Draft, each line linked to maintenance via `jobId` | SA-level bulk PO entry point |
| PO Status column on Maintenance List + SA Maintenances tab | Task material import capability |
| Non-inventory, expense-only flow; Intacct integration | Automated vendor emailing |

---

### Customer Validation — Reed Powell (Mac Miller, March 5, 2026)

| Question | Reed's Answer |
|---|---|
| Must POs group per maintenance? | Not strictly required — line-level traceability to the maintenance job is sufficient |
| How do you pick vendors? | Location-driven — different regions, different vendors for the same item. Set override per contract/location at SA setup. |
| Order all materials or only specific? | Needs flexibility — belts are often "as-needed" post-maintenance, not pre-ordered. Must be able to exclude. |
| Control at what level? | Maintenance level is sufficient — doesn't need to go to the asset level |
| One vendor or multiple per item? | Usually one (volume relationship). Ability to split lines if one can't fulfill the full order. |
| Overall reaction | ✅ *"This is directionally correct for sure… this would be a full-time job for us if you don't have this feature."* |

---

## 9. Bulk PO — Q&A with Engineering/Product

*(Slack thread, March 2026 — Marqui Dixon, Manvi Gupta)*

---

**Q1: Why can't I add default P&M to checklists?**

> **A:** Feature gap — no architectural reason, just hasn't been built. `TaskTemplatePart` only exists in the SA context today, not on library-level checklists. Ideal fix: allow P&M + `includeInBulkPO` flag at the checklist level, propagating to every SA that uses it. On maintenance generation, only items with flag = `true` get picked up.

---

**Q2: Can property tasks carry P&M into an SA if checklists can't?**

> **A:** Not a practical path — and moot for Service Logic specifically. SL's tasks flow from **Quadra via API integration**, bypassing the checklist library entirely. The flag must be settable via the SA task API directly.

---

**Q3: Can we auto-add property tasks to an SA?**

> **A:** Not supported. SA setup is intentional/wizard-driven. For SL, Quadra owns task loading via API — auto-population from checklist templates isn't the right path.

---

**Q4: Do items assigned to assets flow anywhere?**

> **A:** No. Asset item associations are surface-level — "this asset contains 4 filters of x make." Under-baked feature. They don't auto-flow to tasks, SAs, or maintenances. Don't rely on this for Bulk PO.

---

**Q5: Where should `includeInBulkPO` live?**

> **Proposed:** Boolean on `TaskTemplatePart` (SA task-level P&M), overridable at each maintenance task level. Ideal: also settable on the Checklist Library so it propagates to every SA using that checklist.
>
> **Key constraint:** Since SL uses Quadra (not the checklist library UI), the flag must be settable via the **public SA task template API**.

---

**Q6: Is department the right level for vendor resolution?**

> **Original proposal:** Department → Item `preferredVendorId` → warn
>
> **Marqui's feedback:** Flip it — item-level vendor is more specific and should win. Department is a blunt fallback.
>
> **Reed's validation:** Vendor decisions are location-driven. Not all customers model geographic branches as departments. Property address/state may be the better resolution tier — **deferred to Phase 2**.
>
> **Revised order:**
> 1. `Product.preferredVendorId` (most specific)
> 2. Department Preferred Vendor mapping (fallback)
> 3. No vendor → exclude + warn

---

## 10. Open Questions & Risks

| # | Question | Status |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `includeInBulkPO` propagation model — item list vs. task vs. checklist level? | 🔴 Open — needs SA team alignment |
| 2 | Bulk endpoint vs. N separate PO create calls — performance limits? | 🔴 Open — spike in progress (IP-5593) |
| 3 | Vendor resolution: department vs. property state — right granularity? | 🔴 Open — validate with SL |
| 4 | Quadra API compatibility — flag must be settable via public task template API | 🔴 Open — needs Quadra schema validation |
| 5 | Task material import needed before bulk PO can scale | 🟡 Separate initiative — acknowledged blocker |
| 6 | Max items / POs per bulk operation — undefined | 🔴 Open — TBD in TDD |
| 7 | Intacct export timing — when do draft bulk POs sync? | 🟢 Confirmed in-scope for Phase 1 |

---

## 11. Related Links

| DRAFT PRD — IPP-326 (Confluence) | [View PRD](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4324327450) |

ddocEntries.title: Feature Brief
ddocEntries.content:
# Avalara Tax Exemptions — Domain Knowledge (IP-5363)

## Problem

Some customers are tax-exempt (government, nonprofits). When these customers have an exemption certificate in Avalara:
- AR invoices correctly show zero tax ✅
- But BuildOps doesn't **retrieve** or **display** the exemption certificate details
- BuildOps doesn't provide **customer-level AP/AR taxability defaults**
- AP purchases for the same job still require manual configuration

## Why AR Exemptions Cannot Auto-Drive AP

> ⚠️ **Important legal distinction:** AR exemption certificates apply to sales only. AP tax depends on the contractor's resale status, jurisdiction, use vs. resale classification, and vendor behavior. Automatically reusing AR certificates for AP would misapply tax law in many cases.

- An AR exemption certificate tells Avalara: "Do not charge sales tax on invoices issued to this customer."
- It does **NOT** mean the contractor is exempt from paying tax when buying materials
- Correct approach: provide explicit, auditable AP controls (`AP Work Taxability Type`, `Use Taxable`)

## Solution: 4 Workstreams

### Workstream 1 — Retrieve & Store Exemption Certificate from Avalara

- Extend customer Avalara sync to retrieve exemption certificate data
- Store: `certificateId`, `exemptionCertRegion`, `exemptionReason`, `effectiveDate`, `expirationDate`, `status`
- On-demand **"Refresh from Avalara"** action on Customer detail

| Status | Condition |
|---|---|
| ✅ Exempt in Avalara | Valid cert + not expired |
| ⚠️ Certificate Expired/Revoked | Valid cert + expired |
| ❌ No certificate | No cert found |
| ❓ Unknown | Sync failed — show last sync timestamp |

### Workstream 2 — Customer-Level Taxability Fields (New)

| Field | Type | Gating |
|---|---|---|
| `isUseTaxable` | Boolean (default: false) | `ap-taxes` flag |
| `apWorkTaxabilityTypeId` | FK to WorkTaxabilityType | `work-taxability-types` + Avalara |
| `arWorkTaxabilityTypeId` | FK to WorkTaxabilityType | `work-taxability-types` + Avalara |

### Workstream 3 — Defaulting & Inheritance

Customer fields default into: Project, Job, Maintenance Job, Service Agreement. Rule: "Customer fields provide defaults, not locked behavior" — all can be manually overridden.

### Workstream 4 — Exemption Status on Documents

| Document | What's shown |
|---|---|
| PO / Receipt / Bill | Read-only badge: "Exempt in Avalara (AR)" / "No certificate" / "Unknown" |
| AR Invoice | Badge + certificate summary + "Last synced" timestamp |

Display is **informational only** — does not automatically change AP tax calculation.

## Feature Flags

| Flag | Controls |
|---|---|
| `work-taxability-types` | AP/AR Work Taxability Type fields on Customer, Job, SA |
| `ap-taxes` | Use Taxable field + AP tax configuration |

## Non-Goals

- Auto-applying AR exemptions to AP (tax law violation)
- Periodic background sync of certificates (deferred)
- Editing exemption certificates from BuildOps (read-only — managed in Avalara)

## Related Links

- [IP-5363 - Avalara Tax Exemptions PRD](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4260888584)
- [TDD - IP-5363](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/EN/pages/4334288898)
- [IPP-337 - Job Taxability Settings PRD](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4263018514)

ddocEntries.title: Feature Brief
ddocEntries.content:
# Job Taxability Settings — Domain Docs (IPP-337 — PPC)

## Problem

Service contractors operate on a simple, all-or-nothing tax model. PPC's current system (IFS) supports three clear states: **Taxable / Non-Taxable / Exempt**. Today in BuildOps, this is fragmented across multiple steps and configuration points.

PPC's core rule:
> "The job is either taxable or not, and therefore, the things we purchase are the opposite."

| Job Type | AR (Customer Invoice) | AP (Vendor Purchases) |
|---|---|---|
| **Taxable** | Charge sales tax to customer | Tax-exempt (resale certificate) |
| **Non-Taxable** | No sales tax | Taxable (consumption/use tax) |
| **Exempt Customer/Job** | No tax | No tax (government, nonprofit, etc.) |

## Solution: Work Taxability Types

A structured, hierarchical system for controlling AP and AR taxability across the entire job lifecycle.

### New Concept: Work Taxability Type

Configurable value (set in Tax Settings) that controls:
- **Line Taxability Rule:** Always Taxable / Always Non-Taxable / Inherit from Item List

### New Entity Fields

| Entity | New Fields |
|---|---|
| `WorkTaxabilityType` | `lineTaxabilityRule` (ALWAYS_TAXABLE / ALWAYS_NON_TAXABLE / INHERIT) |
| `JobBlueprint` (Job Template) | `workTaxabilityTypeId` (AP) + `arWorkTaxabilityTypeId` (AR) |
| `Job` | `workTaxabilityTypeId` (AP) + `arWorkTaxabilityTypeId` (AR) |
| `ServiceAgreement` | `workTaxabilityTypeId` (AP) + `arWorkTaxabilityTypeId` (AR) |
| `Customer` | `apWorkTaxabilityTypeId` + `arWorkTaxabilityTypeId` |

### Defaulting Hierarchy

```
Customer defaults → Job Template re-defaults → SA re-defaults → Job (manual override)
```

### PO/Invoice Line Behavior

When a PO is created for a job:
- ALWAYS_TAXABLE → all lines `Taxable = true`
- ALWAYS_NON_TAXABLE → all lines `Taxable = false`
- INHERIT → each line inherits from the Item List `taxable` setting

### Feature Flags

| Flag | Controls |
|---|---|
| `work-taxability-types` | Exposes Work Taxability fields on Job Templates, Jobs, SAs, Customers |
| `ap-taxes` | Exposes AP tax configuration settings |

## PPC's Three Core Scenarios

| Scenario | AR | AP |
|---|---|---|
| Taxable Job | Tax collected (sales tax) | Tax-exempt (resale certificate) |
| Non-Taxable Job | Tax-exempt | Taxable (consumption/use tax) |
| Exempt Customer/Job | Tax-exempt | Tax-exempt |

## New Screen: Tax Settings

`Company → Tax Settings` consolidates:
- Work Taxability configuration (when `work-taxability-types` = true)
- Sales Tax list
- AP Taxes configuration (when `ap-taxes` = true and Avalara = false)

## Related Links

- [IPP-337 - Job Taxability Settings PRD](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4263018514)
- [Job Taxability - Meeting Notes (PPC)](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4262461453)

# Vendors & Subcontractors Unified Experience — Domain Docs (IP-5536)

## Problem

The **Subcontractors** page and **Vendors** page live in completely separate places, even though subcontractors are fundamentally a type of vendor. This causes:
- Users switching between two pages for related workflows
- **Divergent data models** — contacts, addresses, notes, and status stored differently for suppliers vs. subcontractors
- Inconsistent archival behavior (Supplier uses "Active" checkbox; Subcontractor uses "Deactivate")
- Duplicate vendor records created when a vendor is both a supplier and a subcontractor

## Proposed Solution

Consolidate into a **single unified Vendors experience** under the Directory menu.

## Key Changes

### Vendor Types (New)

| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| **Supplier** | Existing type. Provides goods/materials. |
| **Subcontractor** | Existing type. Provides labor services. |
| **Supplier & Subcontractor** | ⭐ New type. Eliminates need for duplicate records. |

> ⚠️ **Sage 300 exception:** "Supplier & Subcontractor" type is NOT available for Sage 300/HH2 tenants.

### Unified Vendor List Page (3 tabs)

| Tab | Shows |
|---|---|
| **All** | Every vendor regardless of type |
| **Suppliers** | type = Supplier OR Supplier & Subcontractor |
| **Subcontractors** | type = Subcontractor OR Supplier & Subcontractor |

### New Vendor Detail Page (adaptive by type)

- **Supplier type** → Vendor Info, Billing/Shipping Address, Contact, Notes, POs & Subcontracts tab, Contacts tab, Bills tab
- **Subcontractor or Supplier & Subcontractor** → All of the above + Compliance tab, Retainage Settings, Subcontractor Type, Service Zone, Tags

### Unified Creation Wizard

- **Supplier flow** → Vendor Type, Vendor Name, Use Taxable, Billing Address, Shipping Address, Contact, Notes
- **Subcontractor flow** → All of the above + Subcontractor Type (multi-select), Service Zone (multi-select), Tags (multi-select), Retainage Settings (conditional)
- When a vendor is created with type = Subcontractor or Supplier & Subcontractor → **auto-generates the linked Subcontractor record**

### Auto-generation Field Mapping (Vendor → Subcontractor)

| Subcontractor Field | Source from Vendor |
|---|---|
| Name | `Vendor.name` |
| Email | `Vendor.email` |
| Phone | `Vendor.phone` |
| Business Address | Vendor Billing Address |
| Subcontractor Type | Multi-select from wizard |
| Service Zone | Multi-select from wizard |
| Tags | Multi-select from wizard |
| Status (isActive) | `Vendor.isActive` |
| Contacts | Vendor's primary contact added as `SubcontractorContact` |

### Unified Archive/Unarchive

- Both types now use a single **Archive** action (kebab menu → Archive)
- Sets `Vendor.isArchived = true`
- Confirmation dialogue required
- Type changes that would cause data loss (Subcontractor → Supplier) are **blocked**

## Feature Flag

`subcontracts` FF must be ON. When ON, the new unified Vendors experience replaces both old pages.

## Open Questions

| # | Question |
|---|---|
| 1 | Should edits to the Vendor record after creation sync to the linked Subcontractor record, or is auto-generation a one-time event? |
| 2 | What markup rate applies for Supplier & Subcontractor vendors in financial calculations? |
| 3 | How should Supplier & Subcontractor vendors be categorized in Project Report Services? |

## Related Links

- [Vendors & Subcontractors Unified Experience PRD](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4308467713)
- [IP-5383: Vendor/Subcontractor Unification TDD](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/EN/pages/4332617736)

ddocEntries.title: Feature Brief
ddocEntries.content:
# Pricebook Fee Configuration — Domain Knowledge

## Context

Fee configuration is a **direct dependency of Pricebook Separation**. When Labor and Material pricebooks are decoupled, fees (truck charges, service charges, consumables) no longer cleanly belong to either cost type.

## What Are Fees?

Fees are **revenue-only items** — no cost behind them, no markup calculation, no job costing impact. Examples: Truck charge ($85 flat), Service charge ($75 flat), Torch kit, recovery fee (consumables).

> Contrast with material items: materials carry both a cost and a revenue component. Fee items are pure revenue — they book to their own revenue account, not material revenue.

## Two Approaches Evaluated

### Option 1: Fee Pricebook

| Pro | Con |
|---|---|
| Familiar model | Adds a 3rd pricebook to select |
| Consistent with separation model | Fees may differ only occasionally — overkill for most |

### Option 2: Property-Level Fee Item Library ✅ Current Direction

A centralized **Fee Item Library** organized by category. Properties select one fee item per category.

| Category | Example Items |
|---|---|
| Truck Charge | "Standard Truck Charge $85", "Negotiated SC from $75→$50" |
| Service Charge | "Standard Service Charge $75" |

**Propagation options when a fee item is updated:**
- Update item in central Fee Library → auto-updates all properties referencing it
- OR update per-property independently

| Pro | Con |
|---|---|
| Independent selection — Truck Charge and Service Charge chosen separately per property | Requires new "Fee Item Library" concept |
| Easy to add variants (negotiated rates) | |
| No combinatorial pricebook explosion | |
| Supports centralized bulk updates via controlled propagation | |
| Keeps fees revenue-only and separate from cost components | |

## What Needs to Change

1. Introduce `Fee` as a distinct item type in the Item List
2. Fee items are revenue-only — no `unitCost`, no markup, no job costing impact
3. Fee defaults per property — a property can have a default truck charge + service charge
4. Pricebook UI — Fee tab only appears on pricebooks if the cost type supports it

## Related Links

- [PRD - Truck Fee / Default Fee Enhancements](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4296900616)
- [Fee Configuration Options: Pricebook vs Property-Level](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4316692507)

# Pricebook Separation (Split by Cost Type) — Domain Docs

## Problem

Today, a single pricebook bundles **Labor + Materials + Equipment** pricing together, causing combinatorial explosion.

> Service Logic example: 118 labor rate structures × 42 material markups = **4,956 pricebooks** required. Impossible to maintain.

## Solution: One Pricebook Per Cost Type

Each pricebook has exactly **one cost type**:

| Cost Type | What it covers |
|---|---|
| **Material** | Parts, inventory items, purchased materials |
| **Labor** | Labor rates, hour types, labor groups |
| **Equipment** | Equipment rental, usage rates |

> ⚠️ Multi-cost-type pricebooks will NOT be allowed under the new model. Existing "full" pricebooks remain backward compatible during migration.

## Key Architectural Decisions

### Cost Type Composition
- Each new pricebook selects exactly 1 cost type on creation
- Cost type cannot be changed after creation
- Existing full pricebooks continue working (backward compatibility)

### Job-Level Selection
- When creating a job, user selects up to 3 pricebooks (one per cost type)
- If a full pricebook is selected → populates labor and equipment fields too (backward compat)
- Each entity resolves pricing from the matching cost-type pricebook

### Pricing Resolution Logic

```
Inventory Part added → resolve from Material pricebook
Labor line added    → resolve from Labor pricebook
Equipment added     → resolve from Equipment pricebook
```

### Inheritance Chain

- Invoice created from a job → inherits all three pricebook IDs from the job
- Invoice created from SA → inherits from SA → Customer → Company
- Ad-hoc invoice → inherits from Customer → Company

## What's Out of Scope (Phase 1)

- Splitting out existing pricebooks into separate cost-type books
- Archiving partial pricebooks
- Multi-select vendor types in all reporting surfaces

## Related Links

- [PRD - Splitting Pricebook Components](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4153737329)

ddocEntries.title: Feature Brief
ddocEntries.content:
# Pricebook Bulk Edits — Domain Docs

## Problem

SmartCare manages **400+ pricebooks** across 15 operating companies. When contract terms or labor rates change:
- A single contract amendment may affect **20–50 pricebooks**
- A union rate increase affects **50+ pricebooks**
- Commodity price changes (refrigerant, copper) may require updates **daily or weekly**
- Today: each pricebook must be opened, edited, and saved individually — hours of work per cycle

## Solution: Two Complementary Entry Points

### Option A — Proactive Bulk Edit (Pricebook List)
For planned, top-down changes affecting many known pricebooks:
1. Search/filter pricebooks by name or customer → checkbox multi-select
2. Choose edit type: Material Markup / Labor Rates / Fixed Price Items / Equipment
3. Configure the change in an editing panel (mirrors existing single-PB editor)
4. Confirmation step: shows count + names of affected pricebooks, warns on skipped items
5. Apply → post-save summary shows successes/failures

### Option C — In-Context Propagation
For reactive changes discovered while editing a single pricebook:
- User saves an edit to a markup tier, labor rate, or fixed price item
- System prompts: "Apply these changes to other price books?"
- User opens multi-select panel → chooses additional pricebooks → confirms

> **Recommended: Both A and C** — they serve complementary use cases.

## Four Edit Surfaces

| Surface | Example Use Case |
|---|---|
| **Material markup tiers** | Spartan Nash contract: bump all markup tiers from 15% → 18% across 20 pricebooks |
| **Labor rates** | Union rate increase: Chicago Local 507 journeyman $150 → $155 across 50+ pricebooks |
| **Fixed price items** | Refrigerant price change: update fixed price across all pricebooks that carry this item |
| **Equipment** | HVAC unit pricing update: update fixed price or markup across pricebooks |

## Key Rules

- ✅ Bulk edits apply **only** to explicitly selected pricebooks — no unintended updates
- ✅ Permission-gated — not available to job reviewers or lower-privilege roles
- ✅ If a selected pricebook doesn't have the targeted item → **skip + surface in post-save summary**
- ✅ Confirmation step before applying
- ❌ Not in scope: index cost, bulk pricebook creation/cloning, automated/scheduled updates

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target |
|---|---|
| Time to apply rate change across 20 PBs | < 5 min (from ~60–90 min manual) |
| Price books updated per bulk session | ≥ 10 on average |

## Related Links

- [PRD: Pricebook Bulk Edits](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4344709161)
- [Draft PRD - Bulk Upsert for Pricebook](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/4023386122)

ddocEntries.title: Feature Brief
ddocEntries.content:
# Multiple Vendor Locations — Domain Docs (IP-1002)

## What It Is

BuildOps supports multiple locations per vendor — in production since IP-1002. Each vendor location represents a physical branch with its own contact, billing address, and shipping address.

This is especially important for large national vendors (Trane, Carrier, Ferguson) that operate across multiple US states. The correct vendor location determines:
- Who to contact for the order
- Where the order ships from
- Which bill-to address appears on the PO

## Data Model

```
Vendor
  ├── defaultLocationId (FK → VendorLocation.id)
  └── VendorLocation (1:N)
        ├── id
        ├── vendorId
        ├── name (e.g. "Ferguson Denver", "Ferguson Phoenix")
        ├── billingAddress
        ├── shippingAddress
        ├── contact (name, phone, email)
        └── isDefault (bool)
```

## How It Drives Bulk PO Grouping

Bulk POs are split into separate POs when any of these three dimensions differ:

```
vendorId + vendorLocationId + shippingDestination → ONE PO
```

**Concrete example:** 10 maintenances, all using Ferguson as the filter vendor:

| Maintenances | State | Vendor Location | Ships to | Result |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 7 maintenances | Colorado | Ferguson Denver | CO warehouse | **1 PO** |
| 3 maintenances | Arizona | Ferguson Phoenix | AZ warehouse | **1 PO** |
| **Total** | | | | **2 POs** |

## Phase 1 vs Phase 2

**Phase 1 (current):**
- Default vendor location used (`Vendor.defaultLocationId`) when no override set
- User can override location in the Bulk PO confirmation dialog

**Phase 2 (deferred):**
- Auto-suggest the vendor location whose state matches the property's state
- E.g., if maintenance is in Colorado → auto-suggest Ferguson Denver

## Shipping Destination Options

| Option | Typical Use |
|---|---|
| **Warehouse** | ⭐ Most common — deliver ahead of time, "tagged and bagged" for tech pickup |
| Vendor Location | Pick up from vendor's branch |
| Job / Project Site | Deliver directly to the property address |
| Manually entered | One-off address |

## Related Links

- [IP-1002 Multiple Vendor Locations PRD](https://buildops.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BPD/pages/3461316618)
